holdings.reason: Triple-confluence anchor — full size endorsed. Q1 EPS +24% YoY underpinned by M&A resurgence, rates-trading alpha, and NIM expansion in a higher-for-longer regime, all three independently supportable. RS +24.2 vs SPY, RSI 61, -2.7% off 52-week highs. At 13% the position sits 2pp inside the hard cap; the thesis is durable enough to hold the line. Financials sector net well within limits.

Anchor short at full size — strongest by confluence in the book. 3-to-1 analyst majority overrules the lone Fundamentals dissent decisively. CFO departure plus active CEO search plus ARR guidance cut to free tiers is a management credibility event with lasting multiple-compression implications; this is not a one-quarter earnings miss. The -18.9% five-day distribution is ongoing institutional exit, not exhaustion selling. At -50.7% off highs, the structural AI-disruption re-rating has further runway; dead-cat bounces are add opportunities, not stop-outs.

Trimmed from 12% to 9% — extension and overlap discipline, not thesis retreat. +164% in three months is parabolic; RSI resetting to 57 shows momentum health but not that the move has been digested. Meta 6GW Instinct deal is live revenue and the NVDA share-gain story persists, but it is now consensus-priced. Combined with SMH at 8%, semi gross long lands ~17% — cycle exposure intact without concentration blow-up. The call is right; the old size was not.

Trimmed from 10% to 8% to control AMD overlap. ETF wrapper at 52-week high breakout, RSI 59, RS +52 vs SPY — semis are the undisputed leadership sector and breadth across AMD/MU/TSM confirms a cycle-wide upswing, not a single-name event. 8% alongside AMD at 9% is diversification with leverage to the theme; 10% alongside AMD at 12% was doubling up.

Trimmed from 10% to 7.5% on crowded-short squeeze risk — -35% YTD with legacy longs still distributing means the short side is packed. RSI 41 provides no oversold cushion to absorb a squeeze; any low-bar earnings beat, M&A speculation, or a single credible Agentforce data point compresses the position fast with no exit window. The AI-agent-replaces-CRM narrative is institutional consensus and the downtrend is intact — slow-bleed short with a valid thesis. Sized to survive the squeeze, not to exploit a clean ride.

Full size — defensive quality that earns its allocation by providing genuine diversification from the cyclical semi and fintech longs. RS +32.5 vs SPY, RSI 64, +44.8% in three months reflects institutional rotation into non-cyclical quality amid macro uncertainty. Single analyst, no cross-desk conflict, and the technical setup (dominant momentum, near-zero drawdown from highs) justifies the full single-analyst size of 7%.

Clean unanimous short at full size — no cross-desk conflict anywhere in the book. Premium multiple sustained by a zero-proof-points turnaround narrative against structurally deteriorating unit economics: negative US same-store sales, secular China traffic decline, and a new CEO with no demonstrated execution record. Only -3.5% off 52-week highs is a textbook mispricing — the multiple compresses as each expected proof point fails to arrive. 7% correctly embeds the near-highs timing risk; the fundamental dislocation is unambiguous.

Trimmed from 8% to 6% — lowest-conviction long in the book, sized accordingly. RSI 15 is the most extreme oversold reading in the universe this cycle and the business is intact: 16% YoY revenue growth, $6.8B buyback, ad-tier structurally accretive. Warner fee is a cash windfall misread as negative. The edge is real but the thesis requires the market to voluntarily reconsider a negative narrative with no identified catalyst timeline. 6% holds the contrarian setup; 8% was pricing in a catalyst we cannot yet see.

Reduced from 8% to 5% on two compounding failure modes that together make full size indefensible. First: binary FOMC event risk today — any balanced or mildly dovish Warsh tone reverses the short immediately with no clean exit. Second: correlation failure risk — TLT short is a risk-on bet that loses in a flight-to-quality event precisely when GS, AMD, and SMH are drawing down, hitting the book from both sides simultaneously. May CPI 4.2% headline eliminates the dovish pivot and rate cuts are a 2027 story — the macro view is right. But single-analyst, conv60, live binary event does not justify 8%. At 5% the higher-for-longer thesis is represented at minimum viable size.

Minimum viable size at 3%, halved from 6%. US-Iran ceasefire drains Strait of Hormuz supply-disruption premium and DXY ~99.5 is a real FX headwind to dollar-denominated oil revenues — both thesis legs are valid. But ceasefire durability is entirely exogenous: if the agreement fractures, the position squeezes hard against the book with no forecastable exit. Dropping to zero is defensible as the Risk Manager noted; at 3%, the geopolitical deflation view is expressed with tolerable binary exposure. Held as a macro hedge, not a conviction position.

thesis: The book opens moderately net-long at +8.5% net / 77.5% gross — deliberately not market-neutral, because the weight of evidence (semiconductor cycle leadership, financial earnings acceleration, AI-disruption victim re-ratings) favors a net-long tilt with asymmetric long quality versus short structural decay. The four CIO-proposed trims — AMD parabolic extension, AMD/SMH semi overlap, CRM crowded-short squeeze exposure, and TLT's dual correlation-failure and live FOMC binary — are size corrections, not thesis reversals; every position stays in the book because the underlying call remains valid. ADBE and GS are the anchors; the shorts provide both alpha and partial hedging against the cyclical longs rather than a clean market-neutral offset, which is an intentional choice given the strength of the long-side confluence.
note: Four trims, zero ejections — the Risk Manager improved the book without breaking it. The net-long tilt is earned conviction, not complacency; if the FOMC reads hawkish today, GS, AMD, and TLT short all move in our favour simultaneously and the book is positioned exactly right.
outlook: Warsh FOMC debut today is the immediate binary watch — a hawkish tone validates GS NIM expansion and TLT short simultaneously; any dovish lean pressures both and warrants TLT short reassessment. AMD next earnings will confirm or break the Meta hyperscaler revenue recognition story and determine whether the 9% size holds. ADBE CEO appointment (or prolonged vacancy) is the key multiple-compression catalyst for the anchor short. NFLX needs a market-structure trigger — analyst upgrade, accelerated buyback, or ad-tier beat — to convert the RSI-15 setup into a tradeable catalyst; watching for that without chasing. Ceasefire durability headlines are the XOM stop-loss monitor.

pitches.thesis: Meta 6GW Instinct GPU deal is live revenue — AMD is taking NVDA share in hyperscaler AI capex that persists in ANY rate regime; RSI 57 is healthy consolidation after a 164% 3m run, not overheated.
M&A resurgence + volatile-rates trading revenue + steepening yield curve NIM: GS Q1 EPS was $17.55 vs $14.12 prior year; RSI 61 and only -2.7% off 52-week high means it's still running with fuel.

May CPI at 4.2% headline / 2.9% core kills any dovish pivot — Warsh's FOMC debut today risks hawkish tone; market is now pricing a possible December hike, not a cut, meaning this bond recovery at $85.72 is a trap.
US-Iran ceasefire restores Strait of Hormuz transit, draining the geopolitical risk premium that was propping oil — DXY ~99.5 plus supply normalization is a double headwind for integrated oil majors.

pitches.note: Macro regime is unambiguously higher-for-longer: 4.2% CPI and a hawkish new Fed chair entering today make rate cuts a 2027 story at best, which is bullish for banks and AI capex survivors (AMD) while punishing bonds and anything that was a geopolitical safe-haven trade (energy). Avoided GLD short despite the clear headwinds — RSI 33 signals a tactical bounce risk I won't stand in front of.

pitches.thesis: At 52wk high (0.0% off), uptrend, RSI 59 — not overbought — RS +52 vs SPY; semis are the clear leadership sector and this is a clean uncontested breakout with AMD/MU/TSM all confirming.
Uptrend, RSI 61, RS +24.2 vs SPY, only -2.7% off 52wk high, +36.5% in 3m; financials are the second-strongest sector and GS is the alpha name — institutional flow chasing the group.
Uptrend, RSI 64, RS +32.5 vs SPY, -1.1% off 52wk high, +44.8% in 3m; dominant momentum with near-zero drawdown from highs — money hiding in defensive quality.
Downtrend, -50.7% off 52wk high, RS -30.5 vs SPY, -18.2% in 3m; structurally broken SaaS with no technical floor visible — RSI 33 but broken names don't snap back, they roll.
Downtrend, -38.9% off highs, RS -25.8 vs SPY; dead SaaS — 1m flat (+0.3%) is a pause in the decline not a base, bearish on any relief bounce.
Dead-cat bounce — +7.1%/1m and RSI 53 but still -41.7% off highs, RS -28.3 vs SPY, -16% in 3m; structurally impaired with no catalyst; fade the bounce.

pitches.note: Two-sided tape: semis and financials are the only sectors with clean uptrends and positive RS — own them. Software/SaaS (ADBE, CRM, ORCL, MSFT) is the structural short sector, broken charts across the board. Avoiding shorts on anything RSI <25 (NFLX, IBIT, ETHA) — too oversold for asymmetric risk.

pitches.thesis: Near-monopoly in professional creative software (Creative Cloud) with durable switching costs and strong FCF — a 50.7% drawdown on AI-disruption fear is excessive; Firefly integration keeps professionals in-ecosystem and the 6.8% single-day flush today looks like terminal capitulation, not fundamental deterioration.

RSI 15 and 40% off highs on a business that has definitively won the streaming wars — ad-tier monetization is structurally margin-accretive, password-sharing crackdown lifted ARPU, and live-events content builds stickiness; the selloff is technically driven, not earnings-driven.
Best-in-class enterprise moat (Azure #2 cloud, Office 365 omnipresent, GitHub, Copilot deeply embedded) trading 27.5% off highs with RSI 39 — quality at a genuine discount, not a value trap.

Only 3.5% off 52-week highs despite US same-store sales turning negative, China traffic structurally declining, and a new-CEO turnaround narrative with no proof points yet — premium multiple on deteriorating fundamentals is a textbook short.

Memory is the most violently cyclical semiconductor category — a +130% 3-month run on HBM/AI demand has priced in perfection just as capex scrutiny from hyperscalers builds; when the cycle turns, memory pricing collapses faster than street models assume.

pitches.note: The dominant theme I see: AI hype has created two-tier distortions — quality software franchises (ADBE, NFLX, MSFT) have been indiscriminately sold off on disruption narratives while cyclical chip names (MU +130% in 3m) have been bid to levels that assume a permanent upcycle; the highest-confidence trade is fading SBUX near highs as operational deterioration is plainly visible and the turnaround story has no evidence yet.

pitches.thesis: Story fully broken: dual leadership void (CFO departed June 15 + active CEO search), organic ARR growth guidance cut ~2pp as company retreats to free tiers under competitive AI pressure — market has re-rated from 'AI winner' to 'AI disruption victim' and each bounce is being sold; -50.7% off highs with no floor in sight.

AI-agent-replaces-CRM narrative gaining institutional consensus; stock down 35% YTD near 52-week lows with crowded legacy-software longs still distributing — no credible near-term catalyst to reverse the 'Agentforce saves Salesforce' thesis that has clearly failed to move sentiment.

RSI 15 is the most extreme oversold reading in the universe today; underlying business is intact (Q1: 16% YoY revenue growth, FCF improving, $6.8B buyback authorized) and the $2.8B Warner termination fee is actually a cash windfall — market overcorrected on deal noise, setup for sharp mean reversion bounce.

RSI 77 at all-time highs after a +20% 3-month run; financials are the most crowded long in the book right now on peak rate-cut optimism — if the Fed delays cuts further or credit softens, this unwinds fast with no margin of safety at current levels.

pitches.note: The dominant theme is broken SaaS/enterprise software stories (ADBE, CRM) where the AI-disruption-victim narrative has firmly taken hold and every bounce is a distribution opportunity; the contrarian long is NFLX where sentiment has catastrophically overcorrected relative to fundamentals. Staying out of the semis crowded long (AMD +164% in 3m) despite the intact AI thesis — risk/reward for new entries is poor after that run.

pitches.thesis: Top cross-sectional momentum in universe: 3m RS +152 vs SPY, 3m price +164%, uptrend, RSI 57 (not extended), only -5.7% off 52wk high — cleanest momentum long on the board
Second-best momentum rank: 3m RS +118 vs SPY, +130.5% 3m, uptrend, RSI 64 (healthy), -9.1% off high — confirms the semi cycle is real, not AMD-specific
Financials sector leader by RS (+24.2 vs SPY, 3m +36.5%), uptrend, RSI 61, -2.7% off 52wk high — breadth and price confirm continued outperformance
Worst RS in universe ex-crypto (-30.5 vs SPY), 3m -18.2%, 5d -18.9% (fresh distribution, not exhaustion), downtrend, -50.7% off high, RSI 33 — broken and still falling
Persistent negative momentum: RS -25.8 vs SPY, 3m -13.5%, downtrend, RSI 41 (not oversold enough to fear squeeze), -38.9% off high — slow-bleed short with no catalyst for reversal

pitches.note: Semiconductor cycle is the dominant signal: AMD and MU rank 1-2 in the universe on every momentum metric with RSIs that aren't extended — the trade is to be long the semis into the cycle. On the short side, ADBE's 5-day -18.9% is fresh distribution, not an exhausted selloff — I want to be short into that breakdown. Avoided NFLX short (RSI 15 squeeze risk) and crypto (too oversold on RSI 22-23 for clean shorts).

cio.note: Book opens at 93% gross / +7% net — modestly net long reflecting a regime where semis and financials are structurally leading and higher-for-longer macro creates clear directional edges on both sides. Key conflict resolution: ADBE resolved 3-1 short (structural leadership void + fresh distribution decisively outweigh the Fundamentals capitulation case); MU skipped entirely (Quant long vs Fundamentals short deadlock with no tiebreaker); BAC short skipped (Sentiment bearish call overruled by Macro/Technicals bullish on the financials complex broadly — GS is the cleaner expression); NKE skipped (days-only Technicals pitch, thesis too thin for the book). The paired sector structure — long GS/AMD/SMH vs short ADBE/CRM — creates a natural equity hedge that profits whether the semis-vs-SaaS divergence widens or compresses, while TLT and XOM shorts hedge the macro tail and NFLX/UNH provide uncorrelated alpha sources on opposite ends of the momentum spectrum.

slate.thesis: Highest-confluence long in book — Macro + Technicals + Quant all independently pitch it. Q1 EPS $17.55 (+24% YoY) with M&A resurgence, volatile-rates trading, and yield-curve NIM expansion in a higher-for-longer regime. RS +24.2 vs SPY, uptrend, RSI 61, only -2.7% off 52-week high — financials sector alpha name with durable earnings tailwinds and institutional flow chasing the group.

Dual-analyst confluence (Macro + Quant) with the top conviction score across both. #1 momentum rank in universe (3m RS +152 vs SPY, +164% in 3m) with RSI 57 showing no extension — this is a healthy consolidation after a strong run, not a chase. Meta 6GW Instinct deal is live hyperscaler revenue and a structural share-gain story from NVDA that persists regardless of rate path.

Technicals' highest-conviction long (conv82). Clean breakout at 52-week high, RSI 59, RS +52 vs SPY — semis are the undisputed leadership sector. AMD/MU/TSM breadth across multiple analysts confirms a cycle-wide upswing, not a single-name event. ETF wrapper diversifies individual event risk while capturing the dominant sector rotation.

Dual-analyst confluence (Fundamentals + Sentiment) on a historically extreme technical dislocation — RSI 15 is the most oversold reading in the entire universe this cycle. Underlying business is intact: 16% YoY revenue growth, $6.8B buyback authorized, ad-tier monetization structurally accretive. $2.8B Warner termination fee is a cash windfall misread as negative. Bounce setup with fundamental support, not a turnaround bet.

Technicals-driven defensive quality allocation. RS +32.5 vs SPY, RSI 64, only -1.1% off 52-week high with +44.8% in 3m — institutional money rotating into non-cyclical quality amid macro uncertainty. Sized below AMD/GS as single-analyst but the technical setup (dominant momentum with near-zero drawdown from highs) warrants inclusion.

Strongest short by confluence — Technicals + Sentiment + Quant vote short (3-1) overruling a single Fundamentals long. CFO departed June 15, active CEO search underway, organic ARR guidance cut ~2pp as company retreats to free tiers under AI competitive pressure. Quant flags fresh -18.9% 5-day distribution — ongoing institutional exit, not exhaustion. -50.7% off highs with no technical floor and the narrative re-rated to 'AI disruption victim.' Fundamentals value case overruled.

Triple confluence (Technicals + Sentiment + Quant). AI-agent-replaces-CRM narrative has reached institutional consensus; Agentforce failed to move sentiment and the thesis is now plainly discredited. Stock -35% YTD with crowded legacy longs still distributing — RS -25.8 vs SPY, downtrend, RSI 41 (not oversold enough for squeeze risk). Slow-bleed short with no credible near-term catalyst for reversal.

Macro regime short: May CPI 4.2% headline / 2.9% core eliminates any dovish pivot narrative. Warsh's FOMC debut today risks an explicitly hawkish tone; market now pricing a possible December hike, not a cut. Bond recovery at $85.72 is a sentiment trap in a structurally higher-for-longer rate environment — rate cuts are a 2027 story at best.

Fundamentals short with no cross-analyst disagreement. Only -3.5% off 52-week highs despite US same-store sales turning negative, China traffic in structural decline, and a new-CEO turnaround narrative with zero proof points. Premium multiple on demonstrably deteriorating fundamentals is a textbook mispricing — the multiple compresses as proof-points fail to materialise.

Macro geopolitical risk premium deflation: US-Iran ceasefire restores Strait of Hormuz transit, draining the supply-disruption premium propping integrated majors. DXY ~99.5 adds a FX headwind to dollar-denominated oil revenues. Sized at minimum to reflect single-analyst sourcing and binary headline risk around ceasefire durability.

verdicts.reason: Triple-confluence anchor with durable earnings tailwinds — M&A resurgence, rates-trading alpha, and NIM expansion all independently supportable. At 13% GS sits within 2pp of the 15% hard cap; flag for monitoring but thesis strength justifies full allocation. Financials sector net +13% is well inside the 45% sector limit.

+164% price return in 3 months is a parabolic run — RSI resetting to 57 does not mean the price move has been digested. Momentum can exhaust violently on crowded mega-runners and the hyperscaler deal is now consensus. Additionally, AMD is a top-3 weight in SMH (~9-10% of ETF NAV), so combined AMD + SMH creates ~22% gross semi exposure on the long book at proposed sizes. Trim AMD to 9% to reduce both extension risk and overlap concentration. The share-gain thesis from NVDA remains valid; this is a size adjustment, not a thesis rejection.

Solid ETF wrapper for the semi cycle but direct AMD overlap is not diversification — it amplifies. At 10% SMH + 12% AMD the book carries ~23% gross semi longs before trimming AMD. Reduce SMH to 8%; combined with AMD at 9% the semi gross long settles at ~17-18%, capturing the sector rotation without undue concentration. Breakout and RS thesis intact.

RSI 15 is statistically extreme and the mean-reversion edge is real, but 'oversold can become more oversold' applies until a catalyst defines the floor. No near-term re-rating event is identified — the Warner fee misread thesis requires the market to voluntarily reconsider a negative narrative, which carries no timeline. Conviction at 61 is the lowest among longs. Reduce to 6% to reflect the absence of a catalyst trigger while retaining the contrarian setup.

Defensive quality at 7% provides genuine portfolio diversification from the cyclical semi/fintech longs. Single-analyst but no cross-analyst conflict. RSI 64 is approaching watch territory but the +44.8% 3-month run is institutional rotation into non-cyclical quality, not retail speculation. 7% is sized correctly for single-analyst conviction. Keep.

Strongest short by confluence — 3-to-1 majority overruling a lone Fundamentals dissent is decisive. CFO departure plus active CEO search plus ARR guidance cut is a management credibility event with lasting multiple-compression implications. The -18.9% 5-day distribution is ongoing institutional exit, not exhaustion selling. At -50.7% off highs, dead-cat bounce risk exists but the structural AI-disruption re-rating has further to run. Keep at 12%; monitor for bounces as potential add points.

Crowded-short squeeze risk is materially understated. -35% YTD with legacy longs still unwinding means the short side is already packed; any sentiment shift — M&A speculation, activist entry, a low-bar earnings beat, or a surprise Agentforce data point — compresses the position fast. RSI 41 provides no oversold buffer to absorb a squeeze. The downtrend thesis is valid but the position should not be sized for a clean ride. Reduce to 7.5% to retain short exposure while capping squeeze blowout risk.

Two compounding risks that together make full size unjustifiable. (1) Binary FOMC event risk today: Warsh's debut is a live headline — any balanced or mildly dovish tone reverses the short immediately with no exit opportunity. (2) Macro-hedge correlation failure: TLT short is a risk-ON bet that LOSES in a broad equity risk-off event (flight-to-quality bond rally), precisely when cyclical longs GS, AMD, and SMH are drawing down — the book gets hit from both sides simultaneously. Single analyst, conv60, no cross-desk validation. Reduce to 5% to retain the higher-for-longer macro view while neutralising the tail-event double-loss.

Timing risk of shorting near 52-week highs is real but the fundamental mispricing is textbook: premium multiple sustained by a turnaround narrative with zero proof points against structurally deteriorating unit economics (negative US SSS, China secular decline). Single analyst with no cross-desk disagreement — clean unanimous short. The timing risk is embedded in the 7% size; that is the right size for a single-analyst, near-highs thesis. Keep.

Weakest conviction in the book at 57, single analyst, explicit binary headline risk: ceasefire durability is entirely exogenous and unforecastable. If the US-Iran agreement fractures, Strait of Hormuz premium returns and XOM squeezes hard against a short. DXY headwind is real but cannot anchor the thesis independently of the supply narrative. Halve to 3% — minimum viable size to hold the geopolitical deflation view. If the desk prefers to avoid binary commodity event risk entirely, DROP is defensible.

risk.summary: The slate has structural conviction — GS and ADBE are strong triple-confluence anchors, the semiconductor cycle thesis is coherent, and the gross/net posture is conservative at 93% / +7% proposed. Four positions require trimming before deployment: AMD's +164% parabolic 3-month run creates extension risk RSI alone cannot dismiss; the AMD/SMH overlap loads 22% gross semi longs at full size; TLT short is the stealth book risk — a risk-ON bet that compounds cyclical-long drawdowns in any flight-to-quality event and carries live FOMC binary exposure today; CRM crowded-short squeeze risk is understated at full 10%. After recommended trims (AMD→9%, SMH→8%, NFLX→6%, CRM→7.5%, TLT→5%, XOM→3%), revised gross lands near 77.5% and net near +8.5% — within all hard limits, sector net peaks well below 45%, and tail-risk sources are meaningfully reduced without abandoning the core thesis on any name.

pitches.note: Macro regime is unambiguously higher-for-longer: 4.2% CPI and a hawkish new Fed chair entering today make rate cuts a 2027 story at best, which is bullish for banks and AI capex survivors (AMD) while punishing bonds and anything that was a geopolitical safe-haven trade (energy). Avoided GLD short despite the clear headwinds — RSI 33 signals a tactical bounce risk I won't stand in front of.

pitches.thesis: At 52wk high (0.0% off), uptrend, RSI 59 — not overbought — RS +52 vs SPY; semis are the clear leadership sector and this is a clean uncontested breakout with AMD/MU/TSM all confirming.
Uptrend, RSI 61, RS +24.2 vs SPY, only -2.7% off 52wk high, +36.5% in 3m; financials are the second-strongest sector and GS is the alpha name — institutional flow chasing the group.
Uptrend, RSI 64, RS +32.5 vs SPY, -1.1% off 52wk high, +44.8% in 3m; dominant momentum with near-zero drawdown from highs — money hiding in defensive quality.
Downtrend, -50.7% off 52wk high, RS -30.5 vs SPY, -18.2% in 3m; structurally broken SaaS with no technical floor visible — RSI 33 but broken names don't snap back, they roll.
Downtrend, -38.9% off highs, RS -25.8 vs SPY; dead SaaS — 1m flat (+0.3%) is a pause in the decline not a base, bearish on any relief bounce.
Dead-cat bounce — +7.1%/1m and RSI 53 but still -41.7% off highs, RS -28.3 vs SPY, -16% in 3m; structurally impaired with no catalyst; fade the bounce.

pitches.note: Two-sided tape: semis and financials are the only sectors with clean uptrends and positive RS — own them. Software/SaaS (ADBE, CRM, ORCL, MSFT) is the structural short sector, broken charts across the board. Avoiding shorts on anything RSI <25 (NFLX, IBIT, ETHA) — too oversold for asymmetric risk.

pitches.thesis: Near-monopoly in professional creative software (Creative Cloud) with durable switching costs and strong FCF — a 50.7% drawdown on AI-disruption fear is excessive; Firefly integration keeps professionals in-ecosystem and the 6.8% single-day flush today looks like terminal capitulation, not fundamental deterioration.

RSI 15 and 40% off highs on a business that has definitively won the streaming wars — ad-tier monetization is structurally margin-accretive, password-sharing crackdown lifted ARPU, and live-events content builds stickiness; the selloff is technically driven, not earnings-driven.
Best-in-class enterprise moat (Azure #2 cloud, Office 365 omnipresent, GitHub, Copilot deeply embedded) trading 27.5% off highs with RSI 39 — quality at a genuine discount, not a value trap.

Only 3.5% off 52-week highs despite US same-store sales turning negative, China traffic structurally declining, and a new-CEO turnaround narrative with no proof points yet — premium multiple on deteriorating fundamentals is a textbook short.

Memory is the most violently cyclical semiconductor category — a +130% 3-month run on HBM/AI demand has priced in perfection just as capex scrutiny from hyperscalers builds; when the cycle turns, memory pricing collapses faster than street models assume.

pitches.note: The dominant theme I see: AI hype has created two-tier distortions — quality software franchises (ADBE, NFLX, MSFT) have been indiscriminately sold off on disruption narratives while cyclical chip names (MU +130% in 3m) have been bid to levels that assume a permanent upcycle; the highest-confidence trade is fading SBUX near highs as operational deterioration is plainly visible and the turnaround story has no evidence yet.

pitches.thesis: Story fully broken: dual leadership void (CFO departed June 15 + active CEO search), organic ARR growth guidance cut ~2pp as company retreats to free tiers under competitive AI pressure — market has re-rated from 'AI winner' to 'AI disruption victim' and each bounce is being sold; -50.7% off highs with no floor in sight.

AI-agent-replaces-CRM narrative gaining institutional consensus; stock down 35% YTD near 52-week lows with crowded legacy-software longs still distributing — no credible near-term catalyst to reverse the 'Agentforce saves Salesforce' thesis that has clearly failed to move sentiment.

RSI 15 is the most extreme oversold reading in the universe today; underlying business is intact (Q1: 16% YoY revenue growth, FCF improving, $6.8B buyback authorized) and the $2.8B Warner termination fee is actually a cash windfall — market overcorrected on deal noise, setup for sharp mean reversion bounce.

RSI 77 at all-time highs after a +20% 3-month run; financials are the most crowded long in the book right now on peak rate-cut optimism — if the Fed delays cuts further or credit softens, this unwinds fast with no margin of safety at current levels.

pitches.note: The dominant theme is broken SaaS/enterprise software stories (ADBE, CRM) where the AI-disruption-victim narrative has firmly taken hold and every bounce is a distribution opportunity; the contrarian long is NFLX where sentiment has catastrophically overcorrected relative to fundamentals. Staying out of the semis crowded long (AMD +164% in 3m) despite the intact AI thesis — risk/reward for new entries is poor after that run.

pitches.thesis: Top cross-sectional momentum in universe: 3m RS +152 vs SPY, 3m price +164%, uptrend, RSI 57 (not extended), only -5.7% off 52wk high — cleanest momentum long on the board
Second-best momentum rank: 3m RS +118 vs SPY, +130.5% 3m, uptrend, RSI 64 (healthy), -9.1% off high — confirms the semi cycle is real, not AMD-specific
Financials sector leader by RS (+24.2 vs SPY, 3m +36.5%), uptrend, RSI 61, -2.7% off 52wk high — breadth and price confirm continued outperformance
Worst RS in universe ex-crypto (-30.5 vs SPY), 3m -18.2%, 5d -18.9% (fresh distribution, not exhaustion), downtrend, -50.7% off high, RSI 33 — broken and still falling
Persistent negative momentum: RS -25.8 vs SPY, 3m -13.5%, downtrend, RSI 41 (not oversold enough to fear squeeze), -38.9% off high — slow-bleed short with no catalyst for reversal

pitches.note: Semiconductor cycle is the dominant signal: AMD and MU rank 1-2 in the universe on every momentum metric with RSIs that aren't extended — the trade is to be long the semis into the cycle. On the short side, ADBE's 5-day -18.9% is fresh distribution, not an exhausted selloff — I want to be short into that breakdown. Avoided NFLX short (RSI 15 squeeze risk) and crypto (too oversold on RSI 22-23 for clean shorts).

cio.note: Book opens at 93% gross / +7% net — modestly net long reflecting a regime where semis and financials are structurally leading and higher-for-longer macro creates clear directional edges on both sides. Key conflict resolution: ADBE resolved 3-1 short (structural leadership void + fresh distribution decisively outweigh the Fundamentals capitulation case); MU skipped entirely (Quant long vs Fundamentals short deadlock with no tiebreaker); BAC short skipped (Sentiment bearish call overruled by Macro/Technicals bullish on the financials complex broadly — GS is the cleaner expression); NKE skipped (days-only Technicals pitch, thesis too thin for the book). The paired sector structure — long GS/AMD/SMH vs short ADBE/CRM — creates a natural equity hedge that profits whether the semis-vs-SaaS divergence widens or compresses, while TLT and XOM shorts hedge the macro tail and NFLX/UNH provide uncorrelated alpha sources on opposite ends of the momentum spectrum.

slate.thesis: Highest-confluence long in book — Macro + Technicals + Quant all independently pitch it. Q1 EPS $17.55 (+24% YoY) with M&A resurgence, volatile-rates trading, and yield-curve NIM expansion in a higher-for-longer regime. RS +24.2 vs SPY, uptrend, RSI 61, only -2.7% off 52-week high — financials sector alpha name with durable earnings tailwinds and institutional flow chasing the group.

Dual-analyst confluence (Macro + Quant) with the top conviction score across both. #1 momentum rank in universe (3m RS +152 vs SPY, +164% in 3m) with RSI 57 showing no extension — this is a healthy consolidation after a strong run, not a chase. Meta 6GW Instinct deal is live hyperscaler revenue and a structural share-gain story from NVDA that persists regardless of rate path.

Technicals' highest-conviction long (conv82). Clean breakout at 52-week high, RSI 59, RS +52 vs SPY — semis are the undisputed leadership sector. AMD/MU/TSM breadth across multiple analysts confirms a cycle-wide upswing, not a single-name event. ETF wrapper diversifies individual event risk while capturing the dominant sector rotation.

Dual-analyst confluence (Fundamentals + Sentiment) on a historically extreme technical dislocation — RSI 15 is the most oversold reading in the entire universe this cycle. Underlying business is intact: 16% YoY revenue growth, $6.8B buyback authorized, ad-tier monetization structurally accretive. $2.8B Warner termination fee is a cash windfall misread as negative. Bounce setup with fundamental support, not a turnaround bet.

Technicals-driven defensive quality allocation. RS +32.5 vs SPY, RSI 64, only -1.1% off 52-week high with +44.8% in 3m — institutional money rotating into non-cyclical quality amid macro uncertainty. Sized below AMD/GS as single-analyst but the technical setup (dominant momentum with near-zero drawdown from highs) warrants inclusion.

Strongest short by confluence — Technicals + Sentiment + Quant vote short (3-1) overruling a single Fundamentals long. CFO departed June 15, active CEO search underway, organic ARR guidance cut ~2pp as company retreats to free tiers under AI competitive pressure. Quant flags fresh -18.9% 5-day distribution — ongoing institutional exit, not exhaustion. -50.7% off highs with no technical floor and the narrative re-rated to 'AI disruption victim.' Fundamentals value case overruled.

Triple confluence (Technicals + Sentiment + Quant). AI-agent-replaces-CRM narrative has reached institutional consensus; Agentforce failed to move sentiment and the thesis is now plainly discredited. Stock -35% YTD with crowded legacy longs still distributing — RS -25.8 vs SPY, downtrend, RSI 41 (not oversold enough for squeeze risk). Slow-bleed short with no credible near-term catalyst for reversal.

Macro regime short: May CPI 4.2% headline / 2.9% core eliminates any dovish pivot narrative. Warsh's FOMC debut today risks an explicitly hawkish tone; market now pricing a possible December hike, not a cut. Bond recovery at $85.72 is a sentiment trap in a structurally higher-for-longer rate environment — rate cuts are a 2027 story at best.

Fundamentals short with no cross-analyst disagreement. Only -3.5% off 52-week highs despite US same-store sales turning negative, China traffic in structural decline, and a new-CEO turnaround narrative with zero proof points. Premium multiple on demonstrably deteriorating fundamentals is a textbook mispricing — the multiple compresses as proof-points fail to materialise.

Macro geopolitical risk premium deflation: US-Iran ceasefire restores Strait of Hormuz transit, draining the supply-disruption premium propping integrated majors. DXY ~99.5 adds a FX headwind to dollar-denominated oil revenues. Sized at minimum to reflect single-analyst sourcing and binary headline risk around ceasefire durability.

verdicts.reason: Triple-confluence anchor with durable earnings tailwinds — M&A resurgence, rates-trading alpha, and NIM expansion all independently supportable. At 13% GS sits within 2pp of the 15% hard cap; flag for monitoring but thesis strength justifies full allocation. Financials sector net +13% is well inside the 45% sector limit.

+164% price return in 3 months is a parabolic run — RSI resetting to 57 does not mean the price move has been digested. Momentum can exhaust violently on crowded mega-runners and the hyperscaler deal is now consensus. Additionally, AMD is a top-3 weight in SMH (~9-10% of ETF NAV), so combined AMD + SMH creates ~22% gross semi exposure on the long book at proposed sizes. Trim AMD to 9% to reduce both extension risk and overlap concentration. The share-gain thesis from NVDA remains valid; this is a size adjustment, not a thesis rejection.

Solid ETF wrapper for the semi cycle but direct AMD overlap is not diversification — it amplifies. At 10% SMH + 12% AMD the book carries ~23% gross semi longs before trimming AMD. Reduce SMH to 8%; combined with AMD at 9% the semi gross long settles at ~17-18%, capturing the sector rotation without undue concentration. Breakout and RS thesis intact.

RSI 15 is statistically extreme and the mean-reversion edge is real, but 'oversold can become more oversold' applies until a catalyst defines the floor. No near-term re-rating event is identified — the Warner fee misread thesis requires the market to voluntarily reconsider a negative narrative, which carries no timeline. Conviction at 61 is the lowest among longs. Reduce to 6% to reflect the absence of a catalyst trigger while retaining the contrarian setup.

Defensive quality at 7% provides genuine portfolio diversification from the cyclical semi/fintech longs. Single-analyst but no cross-analyst conflict. RSI 64 is approaching watch territory but the +44.8% 3-month run is institutional rotation into non-cyclical quality, not retail speculation. 7% is sized correctly for single-analyst conviction. Keep.

Strongest short by confluence — 3-to-1 majority overruling a lone Fundamentals dissent is decisive. CFO departure plus active CEO search plus ARR guidance cut is a management credibility event with lasting multiple-compression implications. The -18.9% 5-day distribution is ongoing institutional exit, not exhaustion selling. At -50.7% off highs, dead-cat bounce risk exists but the structural AI-disruption re-rating has further to run. Keep at 12%; monitor for bounces as potential add points.

Crowded-short squeeze risk is materially understated. -35% YTD with legacy longs still unwinding means the short side is already packed; any sentiment shift — M&A speculation, activist entry, a low-bar earnings beat, or a surprise Agentforce data point — compresses the position fast. RSI 41 provides no oversold buffer to absorb a squeeze. The downtrend thesis is valid but the position should not be sized for a clean ride. Reduce to 7.5% to retain short exposure while capping squeeze blowout risk.

Two compounding risks that together make full size unjustifiable. (1) Binary FOMC event risk today: Warsh's debut is a live headline — any balanced or mildly dovish tone reverses the short immediately with no exit opportunity. (2) Macro-hedge correlation failure: TLT short is a risk-ON bet that LOSES in a broad equity risk-off event (flight-to-quality bond rally), precisely when cyclical longs GS, AMD, and SMH are drawing down — the book gets hit from both sides simultaneously. Single analyst, conv60, no cross-desk validation. Reduce to 5% to retain the higher-for-longer macro view while neutralising the tail-event double-loss.

Timing risk of shorting near 52-week highs is real but the fundamental mispricing is textbook: premium multiple sustained by a turnaround narrative with zero proof points against structurally deteriorating unit economics (negative US SSS, China secular decline). Single analyst with no cross-desk disagreement — clean unanimous short. The timing risk is embedded in the 7% size; that is the right size for a single-analyst, near-highs thesis. Keep.

Weakest conviction in the book at 57, single analyst, explicit binary headline risk: ceasefire durability is entirely exogenous and unforecastable. If the US-Iran agreement fractures, Strait of Hormuz premium returns and XOM squeezes hard against a short. DXY headwind is real but cannot anchor the thesis independently of the supply narrative. Halve to 3% — minimum viable size to hold the geopolitical deflation view. If the desk prefers to avoid binary commodity event risk entirely, DROP is defensible.

risk.summary: The slate has structural conviction — GS and ADBE are strong triple-confluence anchors, the semiconductor cycle thesis is coherent, and the gross/net posture is conservative at 93% / +7% proposed. Four positions require trimming before deployment: AMD's +164% parabolic 3-month run creates extension risk RSI alone cannot dismiss; the AMD/SMH overlap loads 22% gross semi longs at full size; TLT short is the stealth book risk — a risk-ON bet that compounds cyclical-long drawdowns in any flight-to-quality event and carries live FOMC binary exposure today; CRM crowded-short squeeze risk is understated at full 10%. After recommended trims (AMD→9%, SMH→8%, NFLX→6%, CRM→7.5%, TLT→5%, XOM→3%), revised gross lands near 77.5% and net near +8.5% — within all hard limits, sector net peaks well below 45%, and tail-risk sources are meaningfully reduced without abandoning the core thesis on any name.

trades.reason: Triple-confluence anchor — full size endorsed. Q1 EPS +24% YoY underpinned by M&A resurgence, rates-trading alpha, and NIM expansion in a higher-for-longer regime, all three independently supportable. RS +24.2 vs SPY, RSI 61, -2.7% off 52-week highs. At 13% the position sits 2pp inside the hard cap; the thesis is durable enough to hold the line. Financials sector net well within limits.

Trimmed from 12% to 9% — extension and overlap discipline, not thesis retreat. +164% in three months is parabolic; RSI resetting to 57 shows momentum health but not that the move has been digested. Meta 6GW Instinct deal is live revenue and the NVDA share-gain story persists, but it is now consensus-priced. Combined with SMH at 8%, semi gross long lands ~17% — cycle exposure intact without concentration blow-up. The call is right; the old size was not.

Trimmed from 10% to 8% to control AMD overlap. ETF wrapper at 52-week high breakout, RSI 59, RS +52 vs SPY — semis are the undisputed leadership sector and breadth across AMD/MU/TSM confirms a cycle-wide upswing, not a single-name event. 8% alongside AMD at 9% is diversification with leverage to the theme; 10% alongside AMD at 12% was doubling up.

Trimmed from 8% to 6% — lowest-conviction long in the book, sized accordingly. RSI 15 is the most extreme oversold reading in the universe this cycle and the business is intact: 16% YoY revenue growth, $6.8B buyback, ad-tier structurally accretive. Warner fee is a cash windfall misread as negative. The edge is real but the thesis requires the market to voluntarily reconsider a negative narrative with no identified catalyst timeline. 6% holds the contrarian setup; 8% was pricing in a catalyst we cannot yet see.

Full size — defensive quality that earns its allocation by providing genuine diversification from the cyclical semi and fintech longs. RS +32.5 vs SPY, RSI 64, +44.8% in three months reflects institutional rotation into non-cyclical quality amid macro uncertainty. Single analyst, no cross-desk conflict, and the technical setup (dominant momentum, near-zero drawdown from highs) justifies the full single-analyst size of 7%.

Anchor short at full size — strongest by confluence in the book. 3-to-1 analyst majority overrules the lone Fundamentals dissent decisively. CFO departure plus active CEO search plus ARR guidance cut to free tiers is a management credibility event with lasting multiple-compression implications; this is not a one-quarter earnings miss. The -18.9% five-day distribution is ongoing institutional exit, not exhaustion selling. At -50.7% off highs, the structural AI-disruption re-rating has further runway; dead-cat bounces are add opportunities, not stop-outs.

Trimmed from 10% to 7.5% on crowded-short squeeze risk — -35% YTD with legacy longs still distributing means the short side is packed. RSI 41 provides no oversold cushion to absorb a squeeze; any low-bar earnings beat, M&A speculation, or a single credible Agentforce data point compresses the position fast with no exit window. The AI-agent-replaces-CRM narrative is institutional consensus and the downtrend is intact — slow-bleed short with a valid thesis. Sized to survive the squeeze, not to exploit a clean ride.

Reduced from 8% to 5% on two compounding failure modes that together make full size indefensible. First: binary FOMC event risk today — any balanced or mildly dovish Warsh tone reverses the short immediately with no clean exit. Second: correlation failure risk — TLT short is a risk-on bet that loses in a flight-to-quality event precisely when GS, AMD, and SMH are drawing down, hitting the book from both sides simultaneously. May CPI 4.2% headline eliminates the dovish pivot and rate cuts are a 2027 story — the macro view is right. But single-analyst, conv60, live binary event does not justify 8%. At 5% the higher-for-longer thesis is represented at minimum viable size.

Clean unanimous short at full size — no cross-desk conflict anywhere in the book. Premium multiple sustained by a zero-proof-points turnaround narrative against structurally deteriorating unit economics: negative US same-store sales, secular China traffic decline, and a new CEO with no demonstrated execution record. Only -3.5% off 52-week highs is a textbook mispricing — the multiple compresses as each expected proof point fails to arrive. 7% correctly embeds the near-highs timing risk; the fundamental dislocation is unambiguous.

Minimum viable size at 3%, halved from 6%. US-Iran ceasefire drains Strait of Hormuz supply-disruption premium and DXY ~99.5 is a real FX headwind to dollar-denominated oil revenues — both thesis legs are valid. But ceasefire durability is entirely exogenous: if the agreement fractures, the position squeezes hard against the book with no forecastable exit. Dropping to zero is defensible as the Risk Manager noted; at 3%, the geopolitical deflation view is expressed with tolerable binary exposure. Held as a macro hedge, not a conviction position.
